items.title: sync/v2 and the 'v2'-ification of Go's standard library
items.content:
— It uses Linux-only network namespaces for now, but this is a Go-powered process-scoped HTTP tracer you can run root privileges. It even decrypts TLS traffic by generating a CA on the fly. Handy for debugging or seeing if dependencies or apps are ‘phoning home,’ perhaps..

Monastic Academy for the Preservation of Life on Earth

items.title: A continued focus on the benefits of Go 1.24
items.content:
• SRCL is a neat new suite of React components for creating webapps with a monospaced, terminal-style aesthetic.

  • If you ever need to work with JavaScript server-side, Bun 1.2 is a huge release for an increasingly popular, high performance -based Node-a-like. It 'just works' for a lot of stuff where Node gives me headaches.

  • Take a look at every byte that goes over the network when making a real Postgres query over TLS . Everything is annotated to help you understand the complexity.

items.title: Go 1.24 Release Candidate 2
items.content:
• VHS 0.9 – CLI 'home video recorder'. "Write terminal GIFs as code for integration testing and demoing your CLI tools." v0.9 adds a keyword for waiting for certain conditions before continuing .

  • 🤖 Mods 1.7 – Charm's pipeline-able AI CLI client. Now with improved GitHub Copilot support and OpenAI o1 support.

  • ☎︎ phonenumbers 1.5 – Go port of Google's libphonenumber library for parsing and validating phone numbers.

  • GoReleaser 2.6 – Build/release binaries for multiple platforms. v2.6 gains Bun and Deno support.

  • Notify 1.3 – Library to send notifications to various services.

  • sqlc 1.28 – Generate type-safe Go code from SQL.

items.title: An interactive tour of Go 1.24
items.content: 📄 Making 'Beautiful' API Keys – Making auth tokens/keys more attractive probably shouldn’t be a priority, but if you want/need to, uuidkey may be for you. Jack Spirou (AgentStation)
